text: Hello! I have globally installed Fuselibs on my Mac in /usr/local/lib/node_modules/fuse-sdk/node_modules/@fuse-open/fuselibs/, but when I make changes there, it does not reflect to the built project even though I run "uno clean" and "uno doctor" in the project folder. I cloned the Fuselibs repo to a separate folder, but how do I assign the project to use that folder instead of the globally installed version?

elements.text: Hello! I have globally installed Fuselibs on my Mac in /usr/local/lib/node_modules/fuse-sdk/node_modules/@fuse-open/fuselibs/, but when I make changes there, it does not reflect to the built project even though I run "uno clean" and "uno doctor" in the project folder. I cloned the Fuselibs repo to a separate folder, but how do I assign the project to use that folder instead of the globally installed version?

text:
Hi,
you need to create  .`unoconfig` file in your project directory to point to the cloned sources of fuselibs.

`.unoconfig` (example content)

```Packages.SourcePaths : [
      ../fuselibs/Source
]```
in the example above the "fuselibs" directory
it's at the same level as the project folder and the path indicated is relative.

it is necessary to execute "uno doctor" after every modification to the source.

check your current configuration by running "uno config" in your project directory.

it might help you further:

<https://forums.fusetools.com/t/step-by-step-installation-fuse/7828>

attachments.fallback: Fuse Community: Step by step installation Fuse
attachments.text: Hi Everybody, i am a newbie in Fuse…can somebody tell step by step to install fuse and another requirement until ready to start code…thanks before…your help so meaningful…i really really want to learn code with fuse

in the example above the "fuselibs" directory
it's at the same level as the project folder and the path indicated is relative.

it is necessary to execute "uno doctor" after every modification to the source.

check your current configuration by running "uno config" in your project directory.

text:
My Uno was 2.9.3 and I cloned the beta3 version of Fuselibs, but after checking out 2.9.2 of Fuselibs in the cloned repo I get error message:

../fuselibs/Source/Fuse.Platform/Fuse.Platform.unoproj: E0100: Package UnoCore was not found ../fuselibs/Source/Fuse.Platform/InterApp.uno(1.7): E3114: There is nothing named Uno accessible in this scope. Are you missing a package reference?

text: I didn't get this to work, but I fixed the openURL method manually from Xcode by editing Fuse.iOS.Bindings.g. Seems kinda dirty, but works. :man-shrugging:
